author: Chase Davis
title: Roki Sasaki shelled early as Dodgers fall 8-2
desc: Roki Sasaki allowed seven earned runs on seven hits and three walks while striking out four over 4.1 innings in Friday's loss to the White Sox.
impact: After the outing, Sasaki now has a 4.76 ERA and 1.33 WHIP with a 3-4 record across 12 starts. He came into this start on a roll over his past four outings, but broke fantasy managers' hearts on Friday night. He gave up five hard-hit balls and picked up 11 whiffs on 91 pitches. His next chance will come against the Orioles.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Shohei Ohtani to make next start Wednesday
desc: Shohei Ohtani is scheduled to make his next start for the Dodgers on Wednesday.
impact: Ohtani is back in the lineup on Saturday following a one-game absence. He won't miss any time on the mound.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Hao-Yu Lee recalled from Triple-A
desc: The Tigers recalled Hao-Yu Lee from Triple-A Toledo.
impact: Lee gets an opportunity in the majors as a depth option in the infield.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Jack Flaherty (peroneal strain) placed on IL
desc: The Tigers placed Jack Flaherty on the 15-day injured list with a left peroneal strain.
impact: Flaherty exited his start early on Friday. He does not hold a clear timetable to return.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Ryan Mountcastle (foot) resumes running
desc: Ryan Mountcastle resumed running in Florida.
impact: Mountcastle is continuing to target his return later in June or in July from a fractured foot.

author: Chase Davis
title: Ivan Herrera chips in two hits, steal in loss
desc: Ivan Herrera went 2-for-5 in a loss to the Twins with a run scored and one stolen base.
impact: Through 67 games, Herrera is batting .265 with seven home runs, 28 RBI, 44 runs scored, three stolen bases, and a .360 wOBA. While he's not making the same contact that made him so interesting last year, his plate discipline is still outstanding (16.4% K%, 12.8% BB%).

author: Ari Koslow
title: Reggie Crawford (shoulder) hopeful to return to mound this summer
desc: Reggie Crawford is still rehabbing his shoulder and the Giants hope he's back on a mound this summer.
impact: Crawford remains without a clear timetable to return.

author: Chase Davis
title: Jordan Walker drives in three, but Cards come up short
desc: Jordan Walker went 1-for-5 against the Twins with a double, three RBI and one strikeout.
impact: After Friday's loss to Minnesota, Walker is batting .300 with 17 home runs, 55 RBI, 45 runs scored and 10 stolen bases through 66 games. After striking out only 16% of the time in May, he's nearly doubled that number through 10 June games. Despite the strikeouts, he's batting .348 with two homers, six runs, 12 RBI, three steals and a 93.4 mph average exit velocity.

author: Chase Davis
title: Alec Burleson homers for fourth straight day
desc: Alec Burleson went 1-for-4 in Friday's loss to the Twins with a home run, one walk, two runs scored, two RBI and two strikeouts.
impact: After Friday's loss to Minnesota, Burleson is batting .290 with 12 home runs, 50 RBI, 37 runs scored and two stolen bases across 66 games. He's homered in four straight games and has seven RBI during this run. He's up to sixth in the league in RBI and second on his team in home runs.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Nick Solak has contract selected by Padres
desc: The Padres selected the contract of INF/OF Nick Solak from Triple-A El Paso.
impact: Solak will hold a bench role for San Diego with Miguel Andujar placed on the injured list.

author: Chase Davis
title: Ryne Stanek coughs up late lead in loss to Twins
desc: Ryne Stanek allowed three earned runs on three hits while striking out one batter to take the loss against the Twins.
impact: After Friday's loss, Stanek now has a 5.60 ERA and 1.61 WHIP with a 10.9 K/9, one save and 11 holds across 30 appearances. He gave up three home runs, including the three-run game-tying homer in the seventh. He's out of contention for any traditional save opportunity, and he's too much of an ERA risk in saves plus holds leagues.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Miguel Andujar (hamstring) placed on IL
desc: The Padres placed DH Miguel Andujar on the 10-day injured list with a left hamstring strain.
impact: Andujar had been hopeful to avoid a stint on the injured list, but he was unable to do so. He does not hold a clear timetable to return.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Michel Otanez designated for assignment
desc: The Rangers designated RHP Michel Otañez for assignment.
impact: Otanez loses his spot on the Rangers 40-man roster. He will head back to waivers.

author: Ari Koslow
title: Luis Curvelo optioned to Triple-A
desc: The Rangers optioned RHP Luis Curvelo to Triple-A Round Rock.
impact: Curvelo will return to Triple-A with Joe Ross having his contract selected by the Rangers.
